title: Pungent Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Moroccan stew made with fermented lamb, juniper berries, and a variety of spices, often served as a hangover cure.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and translucent.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the fermented lamb shoulder and cook until it is browned on all sides.
Add the juniper berries, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Pour in 2 cups of water and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Use a match or lighter to flambé the tagine, being careful not to splash any of the hot liquid.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
